metadata.from: James E. Hanifen (Sr. or Jr. — the registered owner is 'J. E. Hanifen' at 424 Virginia, Des Moines, Iowa; the signature on the certification reads 'James E. Hanifen'; the back of the envelope is stamped/printed 'Hanifen Body & Paint Co.' and signed 'James E. Hanifen 4/8/22-3' — this is almost certainly James Edward Hanifen Sr., the father, who ran the body and paint shop)
metadata.to: N/A — this is not a letter but a WWII-era gasoline ration book envelope and ration book
metadata.date: 4/8/22-3 (written on back — likely a date notation, possibly April 8 or year model reference)
metadata.envelope_notes: This is a Gasoline Ration Book Envelope (not a mailed letter). Front cover reads 'Gasoline Ration Book Envelope' with handwritten annotation '41 Ply' (1941 Plymouth). Back reads: 'Conserve Your Car... have dented, scratched or rusted areas refinished—damaged sections repaired. For this service, bring your car to— HANIFEN BODY & PAINT CO. 904 Keo Way, Des Moines, Iowa. Compliments of MINNESOTA MINING & MFG. CO., St. Paul, Minn.' Handwritten on back: '41 Ply' and signature 'James E. Hanifen 4/8/22-3'. Inside pocket has handwritten notation '41 Ply - 37'. The ration book inside is OPA Form R-525C, 'A Basic Mileage Ration', United States of America, Office of Price Administration. Registered owner: J. E. Hanifen, 424 Virginia, Des Moines, Iowa. License No. 77-33990 (Iowa). Year Model and Make: Ply[mouth] 41 [Iowa?]. Serial number on coupons: 584736. Several 'A17' mileage coupons visible; some coupons appear to have been used/torn. Back of ration book has 'Certification of Bookholder' signed by James E. Hanifen, address 424, Virginia Ave. Notice instructs holder to write car license number and state on each coupon.
metadata.mood_tone: Administrative/official — a wartime rationing document, not a personal letter
metadata.legibility_notes: Handwriting is fairly clear. The date on back '4/8/22-3' is somewhat ambiguous — may refer to a date (April 8) combined with a model year notation, or another reference.
metadata.historical_context: WWII-era gasoline rationing. The Office of Price Administration (OPA) issued 'A' Basic Mileage Ration books to civilian car owners, limiting gasoline consumption as part of the wartime effort. The 'A' card was the lowest-priority ration, allotting approximately 3-4 gallons per week for non-essential driving. The envelope was produced and distributed by Hanifen Body & Paint Co. as a promotional item, compliments of Minnesota Mining & Manufacturing Co. (3M).

pages.transcription:
Gasoline Ration Book Envelope
[handwritten:] 41 Ply

DIRECTIONS: Open book and place back cover and pages of coupons in pocket of envelope, except the top cover and page in current use. See cut. This prevents tearing off coupons from more than one page at a time.

[Inside of folded envelope showing two pockets. Handwritten notation in center fold:]
41 Ply - 37

[Bottom printed text:]
PATENT PENDING
U. S. ENVELOPE CO.

IMPORTANT INSTRUCTIONS
1. Coupons can be used only in connection with the vehicle described on the front cover. Detached coupons are VOID.
2. If you stop using your car, this book and all unused coupons must be surrendered to your Board within 5 days.
3. If you sell your car, this book and all unused coupons must be surrendered to your Board. The purchaser will not be issued a gasoline ration unless he presents the receipt which you receive at the time of such surrender.
☆ OPO

[Right side:]
A  BASIC MILEAGE RATION
UNITED STATES OF AMERICA
OFFICE OF PRICE ADMINISTRATION

NAME OF REGISTERED OWNER
J. E. Hanifen

ADDRESS—NUMBER AND STREET
424 — Virginia

CITY AND STATE
Des Moines, Iowa

LICENSE NO. AND STATE       YEAR MODEL AND MAKE
77-33990                    Ply[mouth] 41 [Iowa?]

Holder must fill in any blank spaces above before the first purchase of gasoline.

[Coupon sheet below — multiple coupons visible, each reading:]
OPA FORM R-525C
BA [License No.]
MILEAGE
A 17
584736
[License No.] [State]

[Seven coupons visible: A17 (×5 top row), A16 and A[?] bottom row — some appear torn/used]

CERTIFICATION OF BOOKHOLDER
(The coupons in this book are not valid unless the lawful holder has signed the certificate below)

I, the undersigned, do hereby agree that I will use this coupon book only in accordance with the rules and regulations of the Office of Price Administration. Each use of the book will constitute a representation to the Office of Price Administration that the book was lawfully acquired and is being lawfully used.

Signature: James E. Hanifen
Address: 424, Virginia Ave

[Box:]
Violators of the gasoline rationing regulations are subject to revocation of rations and criminal prosecution under the laws of the United States

NOTICE

YOU MUST IMMEDIATELY WRITE OR STAMP YOUR CAR LICENSE NUMBER AND STATE OF REGISTRATION ON THE FACE OF EACH COUPON.

Otherwise these coupons are INVALID and may be revoked.

[Box:]
Any person finding a lost coupon book should mail it or return it at once to the nearest War Price and Rationing Board

Conserve Your Car . . . have dented, scratched or rusted areas refinished—damaged sections repaired. For this service, bring your car to—

[handwritten:] 41 Ply

HANIFEN BODY & PAINT CO.
904 Keo Way
Des Moines, Iowa

[handwritten:] James E. Hanifen  4/8/22-3.

Compliments of MINNESOTA MINING & MFG. CO., St. Paul, Minn.

full_transcription:
FRONT:
Gasoline Ration Book Envelope
[handwritten:] 41 Ply
DIRECTIONS: Open book and place back cover and pages of coupons in pocket of envelope, except the top cover and page in current use. See cut. This prevents tearing off coupons from more than one page at a time.

INSIDE:
[Handwritten:] 41 Ply - 37
PATENT PENDING / U. S. ENVELOPE CO.

RATION BOOK (FRONT):
IMPORTANT INSTRUCTIONS
1. Coupons can be used only in connection with the vehicle described on the front cover. Detached coupons are VOID.
2. If you stop using your car, this book and all unused coupons must be surrendered to your Board within 5 days.
3. If you sell your car, this book and all unused coupons must be surrendered to your Board. The purchaser will not be issued a gasoline ration unless he presents the receipt which you receive at the time of such surrender.

A BASIC MILEAGE RATION
UNITED STATES OF AMERICA / OFFICE OF PRICE ADMINISTRATION
NAME OF REGISTERED OWNER: J. E. Hanifen
ADDRESS: 424 — Virginia
CITY AND STATE: Des Moines, Iowa
LICENSE NO.: 77-33990   YEAR MODEL AND MAKE: Plymouth 41
Holder must fill in any blank spaces above before the first purchase of gasoline.
[Coupons: OPA FORM R-525C, A17 mileage coupons, serial 584736]

RATION BOOK (BACK):
CERTIFICATION OF BOOKHOLDER
I, the undersigned, do hereby agree that I will use this coupon book only in accordance with the rules and regulations of the Office of Price Administration. Each use of the book will constitute a representation to the Office of Price Administration that the book was lawfully acquired and is being lawfully used.
Signature: James E. Hanifen
Address: 424, Virginia Ave
Violators of the gasoline rationing regulations are subject to revocation of rations and criminal prosecution under the laws of the United States.
NOTICE: YOU MUST IMMEDIATELY WRITE OR STAMP YOUR CAR LICENSE NUMBER AND STATE OF REGISTRATION ON THE FACE OF EACH COUPON. Otherwise these coupons are INVALID and may be revoked.
Any person finding a lost coupon book should mail it or return it at once to the nearest War Price and Rationing Board.

ENVELOPE BACK:
Conserve Your Car . . . have dented, scratched or rusted areas refinished—damaged sections repaired. For this service, bring your car to—
[handwritten:] 41 Ply
HANIFEN BODY & PAINT CO.
904 Keo Way, Des Moines, Iowa
[handwritten:] James E. Hanifen  4/8/22-3.
Compliments of MINNESOTA MINING & MFG. CO., St. Paul, Minn.
summary: This item is not a personal letter but a WWII-era gasoline ration book and its envelope, belonging to James E. Hanifen Sr. of 424 Virginia Ave., Des Moines, Iowa. The 'A Basic Mileage Ration' book (OPA Form R-525C) was issued for his 1941 Plymouth (Iowa license 77-33990) and contains several unused 'A17' mileage coupons with serial number 584736. The envelope was a promotional item from Hanifen Body & Paint Co. (904 Keo Way, Des Moines), distributed compliments of Minnesota Mining & Manufacturing Co. (3M), suggesting James Sr. owned or operated an auto body shop — consistent with the family business context. This document provides important evidence of the Hanifen family's wartime home-front experience and their connection to the automobile repair trade in Des Moines.
